top_10.rationale: Official Python documentation — Event Loop reference. The canonical primary source: event loop is the core of every asyncio application, covers run/stop/callbacks/subprocesses.
Real Python 'Python's asyncio: A Hands-On Walkthrough' — comprehensive tutorial covering coroutines, event loops, awaitable objects, and concurrent I/O-bound task management.
'A Web Crawler With asyncio Coroutines' from Architecture of Open Source Applications — classic foundational tutorial showing asyncio event loop concurrency in practice.
'Python Event Loop' — tutorial specifically focused on the Python event loop and how Python uses it to achieve the concurrency model using a single thread.
SuperFastPython 'Asyncio Event Loop in Separate Thread' — covers running event loops in background threads using asyncio.run_coroutine_threadsafe(). Practical concurrency pattern.
'Python AsyncIO: Event Loop Internals and Practices' — covers event loop internals and practical patterns for I/O-bound workloads in Python asyncio.
SuperFastPython 'Asyncio Run Multiple Concurrent Event Loops' — covers running multiple asyncio event loops concurrently by starting each in a separate thread.
Educative 'Python Concurrency: Making sense of asyncio' — explains asyncio event loop concurrency model on a single thread, how it switches between tasks at await points.
'Advanced Python Concurrency: Asyncio Event Loop Optimization and Concurrent Futures Patterns' — deep guide on optimizing the event loop for high-performance I/O-bound and mixed workloads.
'Asyncio Architecture in Python: Event Loops, Tasks, and Futures Explained' — covers event loop architecture, single-threaded concurrency, and how asyncio manages I/O waiting.
